algae_description: Dense floating plant coverage across much of the water surface — appears to be duckweed or frogbit, creating a nearly continuous green mat. Some green growth also visible low on the substrate near the front-left, likely low-growing stem or rosette plants.
substrate_condition: Dark granular substrate (appears to be aqua soil or similar), relatively even coverage, no visible debris accumulation from this angle.
concerns: Surface plant coverage is very dense — may be reducing gas exchange and light penetration to mid/low plants if left unchecked.
Water level appears slightly low relative to tank rim — possibly evaporation loss worth topping off.
narrative: Media Luna on Day 31 is looking quietly alive — water reads clear, a dual-arm grow light is running, and the surface is thick with floating plants that have clearly taken off. Two small reddish shrimp are just barely visible near the substrate front-left, suggesting at least some colony members are comfortable enough to be out in the open.
